title: Dual Array Trie
content: Dual Array Tries are compact trie structures used for prefix search and autocomplete. They store transitions in base and check arrays.

title: Trie Data Structure
content: A trie stores strings character by character. Tries support efficient prefix search and autocomplete operations.

title: Inverted Index
content: An inverted index maps terms to documents. Search engines use inverted indexes for fast document retrieval.

title: BM25 Ranking
content: BM25 ranks documents using term frequency, document frequency, and document length normalization.

title: Prefix Search
content: Prefix search finds terms that begin with a given prefix. Tries are commonly used for prefix search.

title: Autocomplete Systems
content: Autocomplete systems use tries or finite state structures to suggest words while users type queries.

title: Hash Tables
content: Hash tables provide constant time average lookup, insertion, and deletion using hash functions.

title: Binary Search Tree
content: Binary search trees maintain sorted data and support logarithmic search, insertion, and deletion.

title: Dijkstra Algorithm
content: Dijkstra algorithm finds shortest paths in weighted graphs using a priority queue.

title: Graph Traversal
content: Depth first search and breadth first search are fundamental graph traversal algorithms.

title: Search Engine Architecture
content: Search engines tokenize documents, build inverted indexes, rank results, and provide autocomplete.

title: Document Tokenization
content: Tokenization splits text into searchable terms. Most search engines lowercase and normalize tokens.

title: AVL Trees
content: AVL trees are self-balancing binary search trees that maintain logarithmic height through rotations.

title: Red Black Trees
content: Red black trees are balanced search trees used in many standard library implementations.

title: B Trees
content: B trees are optimized for disk storage and are commonly used in databases and file systems.

title: Dynamic Programming
content: Dynamic programming solves problems by storing solutions to overlapping subproblems.

title: Merge Sort
content: Merge sort is a divide and conquer sorting algorithm with O(n log n) time complexity.

title: Quick Sort
content: Quick sort partitions arrays around a pivot and performs efficiently on average.

title: Heap Data Structure
content: A heap is a complete binary tree used to implement priority queues efficiently.

title: Finite State Automata
content: Finite state automata are used in text processing, lexical analysis, and efficient string matching.
